title: hibbertia suffrutescens
intro: Hibbertia suffrutescens, also known as the straggly guinea flower, is a type of flowering plant native to Western Australia. The leaves and stems of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Hibbertia suffrutescens contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which may provide health benefits such as reducing pain and inflammation, and supporting the immune system. The plant is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and in some cases, the leaves are dried and used in herbal remedies or added to skincare products.
